title: marsilea coromandelica
intro: Marsilea coromandelica, also known as Cheeku, is a perennial aquatic plant native to the Indian subcontinent, belonging to the water clover or devil's claw family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Marsilea coromandelica include its ability to treat fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural diuretic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or powder, and can be applied topically to treat wounds and skin irritations.
